mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of Kubernetes clusters on AWS. It is ideal for users who want to leverage AWS's infrastructure while maintaining control over their Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS services and potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the AWS Command Line Interface, which is necessary for managing AWS services.

```bash

pip install awscli==1.22.0

```

> Use pip to install a specific version of AWS CLI.

steps.title: Create an EKS Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321 --region us-west-2

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID with your actual AWS account ID.
commands: aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

```

> Ensure you are using the correct region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your EKS cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/aws/amazon-cloudwatch-agent/main/quickstart/amazon-cloudwatch-agent.yaml

```

> This configures the cluster autoscaler.
> This configures CloudWatch monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster --region us-west-2

```

uninstall.title: Cleanup Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This command cleans up any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs that may remain after cluster deletion.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?contains(LoadBalancerName, `my-cluster`)].LoadBalancerArn' --region us-west-2 | xargs -I {} aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn {}

```
commands: aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[?contains(LoadBalancerName, `my-cluster`)].LoadBalancerArn' --region us-west-2 | xargs -I {} aws elbv2 delete-load-balancer --load-balancer-arn {}

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data and configurations. This command upgrades the EKS cluster to a new version.

```bash

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --region us-west-2

```

> Backup your data before running this command.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the nodes are running the new version.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check that nodes are running the upgraded version.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Configuration
upgrade.description:
Reapply any configurations that may have been affected by the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

```

> Reapply autoscaler configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the EKS cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster --region us-west-2' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: No resources found in namespace kube-system
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the necessary components may not be deployed.

**Fix:**
Check if the EKS cluster is active and components are deployed using 'kubectl get all -n kube-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Cluster autoscaler not scaling
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the IAM role does not have the necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the IAM role associated with the cluster has the 'Auto Scaling' permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Insufficient capacity in your availability zone
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available in the specified availability zone.

**Fix:**
Try launching the cluster in a different availability zone or check your resource limits.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved IAM roles for service accounts.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of EC2 instance types that are not supported in the latest Kubernetes version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.30, ensure to review the IAM roles and permissions.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get nodes' showing nodes in 'Ready' state, confirming that the EKS cluster is operational.
codeSnippets: aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and AWS account set up before proceeding.
